user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: VanArsdel Ltd.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-14 13:43 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 08:36 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 10:15 EST
Subject: URGENT: partner engagement Issue at VanArsdel Ltd

There's been a change in our timeline for partner engagement. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:07:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:15:00] Kevin (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for VanArsdel Ltd: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-A trap (Classifies internal-only meeting as customer meeting); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-A failure mode (Classifies internal-only meeting as customer meeting)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-A -- Classifies internal-only meeting as customer meeting
